title: Drooling and Stridor - Epiglottitis
chief_complaint: 4-year-old with acute onset high fever 40°C, severe sore throat, drooling, muffled voice, and inspiratory stridor. Sitting in tripod position, refusing to lie down. Unvaccinated against Hib.

physical_exam.general: Toxic-appearing, anxious, sitting forward in tripod position, drooling, inspiratory stridor

key_learning_points: Tripod position + drooling + stridor = epiglottitis until proven
DO NOT examine throat (may precipitate complete obstruction)
Hib vaccine has dramatically reduced incidence

patient_responses.symptoms: Started with fever this morning, now he can't swallow his saliva

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Epiglottitis is a life-threatening airway emergency.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is epiglottitis. The tripod position + drooling + stridor are classic.

cxr_lateral_neck.comment: Thumb sign - but do NOT delay airway management for imaging.

feedback.attending_note: Epiglottitis: Secure airway in OR with anesthesia and ENT. Do NOT examine throat, do NOT lay child flat, do NOT cause agitation. IV ceftriaxone 50mg/kg/day. Steroids controversial. Extubate when leak demonstrated (24-48h). Hib vaccine prevents this disease.
